docs.title: 30sundays Tech Docs
docs.text: Welcome to the 30sundays Technical Documentation 📚 This site is the central place for documenting all our projects, APIs, workflows, and engineering practices.

docs.title: 🔹 How to Contribute Documentation
docs.text: All documentation is written in Markdown (.md) files inside the docs/ folder. Every file you add here becomes a page in the site automatically when we build with MkDocs.

docs.title: Workflow for Developers
docs.text:
1. Clone the repository bash git clone https://github.com/<org>/<repo>.git cd <repo>

  2. Install dependencies bash pip install -r requirements.txt

  3. Run locally bash mkdocs serve Open http://127.0.0.1:8000 in your browser to preview docs with live reload.

  4. Add or edit documentation

  5. Write content in Markdown (.md) format.
  6. Place your files in the correct folder (see structure below).

  7. Use code blocks for examples, e.g.: python def example(): print("Hello, Docs!")

  8. Commit and push bash git checkout -b docs/update-auth git add docs/api/authentication.md git commit -m "docs(api): add authentication docs" git push origin docs/update-auth Then open a Pull Request for review.

docs.title: 📂 Project Documentation Structure
docs.text:
We follow this folder layout under docs/:

docs/
│
├── index.md                # Homepage (this page)
│
├── guides/                 # How-to guides for developers
│   ├── setup.md            # Setup instructions
│   ├── deployment.md       # Deployment guide
│   └── troubleshooting.md  # Common issues
│
├── api/                    # API documentation
│   ├── overview.md
│   ├── authentication.md
│   └── users.md
│
├── frontend/               # Frontend-specific docs
│   ├── architecture.md
│   └── components.md
│
├── backend/                # Backend-specific docs
│   ├── services.md
│   └── database.md
│
└── assets/                 # Images, diagrams, screenshots
    └── architecture.png

docs.title: 🛠️ Useful Commands
docs.text:
• mkdocs serve – Start the live-reloading docs server.
  • mkdocs build – Build the static documentation site into site/.
  • firebase deploy – Deploy to Firebase Hosting (if configured).

docs.title: ✅ Best Practices
docs.text:
• Keep docs close to the code they describe.
  • Use clear file names (auth.md, setup.md) for easy navigation.
  • Use screenshots, diagrams, and examples wherever helpful.
  • Always create a Pull Request for changes so others can review.
